title: Elatostema caulialatum
intro: Elatostema caulialatum, also known as Caulialatum Polygonum or Caulialatum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Elatostema caulialatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used in the form of tablets or capsules, making it a popular ingredient in herbal supplements.
taxonomy: Elatostema caulialatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant is native to the island of Sulawesi in Indonesia. The classification of Elatostema caulialatum is as follows: it belongs to the kingdom Plantae, the order Urticales, the family Urticaceae, the genus Elatostema, and the species E. caulialatum. The genus Elatostema is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "False Daisy" or "False Nettle." Elatostema caulialatum is one of the many species within this genus.
